verdict: sonnet-no-mcp mis-mapped the repository and recovered only shallow fragments of the Angular single-choice rule
reason: The answer opens by declaring the repository non-Angular, then answers from generic framework knowledge with a wrong microtask-based mechanism. It keeps a small amount of comparator and batching intuition but misses the queued post-render rule, teardown injector choice, destruction guard, and multi-select contrast that make the candidate uniquely grounded.
notes: This run is strong hardness evidence because Sonnet failed to enter the benchmark-data Angular code path and fell back to a knowledge answer instead.
No valid-but-unkeyed alternative facts were recovered.
